prompt: Ada Lovelace worked with a mathematician who designed the Difference Engine. What was that mathematician's last name?

contexts.title: Ada Lovelace
contexts.text: Ada Lovelace was an English mathematician and writer, chiefly known for her work on Charles Babbage's proposed mechanical general-purpose computer, the Analytical Engine.

contexts.title: Charles Babbage
contexts.text: Charles Babbage was an English mathematician, philosopher, inventor, and mechanical engineer. He originated the concept of a digital programmable computer and designed the Difference Engine.

prompt: The spacecraft MESSENGER orbited which planet, the one closest to the Sun?

contexts.title: MESSENGER
contexts.text: MESSENGER was a NASA robotic space probe that orbited the planet Mercury between 2011 and 2015 to study its chemical composition, geology, and magnetic field.

contexts.title: Mercury (planet)
contexts.text: Mercury is the smallest planet in the Solar System and the closest to the Sun.
